ocr.text: The report analyzes the effects of various antidepressants on Cx43 expression and function across multiple studies.
Amitriptyline, a TCA, increases both mRNA and protein levels of Cx43 in primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a
treatment dose of 25 μM for 48 hours, as noted by Morioka et al., 2014. Clomipramine, also a TCA, increases Cx43 protein in
primary cultures of rat cortical astrocytes with a treatment dose of 10 μM for 48 hours, per Morioka et al., 2014. Fluoxetine,
classified as an SSRI, shows an increase in protein in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex at a dosage of 20 mg/kg i.p.
Antidepressant  Class  Effect on Cx43 expression  Effect on Cx43 function  Model  Treatment (dose and time)  Reference
Amitriptyline  TCA  Increase (mRNA, protein)  Increase (gap junction)  Primary cultures rat cortical astrocytes  25 μM, 48 h  Morioka et al., 2014
Clomipramine  TCA  Increase (protein)  NT  Primary culture rat cortical astrocytes  10 μM, 48 h  Morioka et al., 2014
Fluoxetine  SSRI  Increase (protein)  NT  Rat in vivo Prefrontal cortex  20 mg/kg i.p. for 21 days  Fatemi et al., 2008
Fluoxetine  SSRI  Increase (mRNA, protein)  NT  Human astrocytoma cell line  10, 20 μg/ml 24 h  Mostafavi et al., 2008
Fluoxetine  SSRI  Increase (protein)  No effect (gap junction)  Rat in vivo Prefrontal cortex  10 mg/kg 21 days  Sun et al., 2012
Duloxetine  SNRI  Increase (mRNA, protein)  No effect (gap junction)  Rat in vivo Prefrontal cortex  10 mg/kg 21 days  Sun et al., 2012
Fluvoxamine  SSRI  Increase (protein)  NT  Primary cultures rat cortical astrocytes  25 μM 48 h  Morioka et al., 2014
US Collaborative  French  Belgian  CIBMTR
Patients  59  87  50  67
Disease Recurrence.  8.5%  8%  10%  13%
Event Free survival  85%  85%  86%  82%
Patients with Chronic GvHD  12%  12.6%  20%  22%
for 21 days according to Fatemi et al., 2008. In another study, it increases both mRNA and protein in a human astrocytoma cell
line with doses of 10 and 20 μg/ml for 24 hours, as shown by Mostafavi et al., 2008. Additionally, Fluoxetine at 10 mg/kg for
21 days results in increased protein but no effect on gap junctions in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex, as noted by Sun et al., 2012.
Duloxetine, an SNRI, elevates mRNA and protein levels without affecting gap junctions in a rat in vivo prefrontal cortex at 10
mg/kg for 21 days, also per Sun et al., 2012. Fluvoxamine, another SSRI, increases protein in primary cultures of rat cortical
astrocytes with a 25 μM dose for 48 hours, according to Morioka et al., 2014. In Table 2, the US Collaborative sample consisted
of 59 patients with a median age of 9.9 (3.3 – 15.9), while the French, Belgian, and CIBMTR cohorts had 87 patients (median
age 9.5 (2–22)), 50 patients (median age 7.5 (0.9 – 23)), and 67 patients (median age 10 (2–27)), respectively. Disease
recurrence rates were 8.5% in the US, 8% in France, 10% in Belgium, and 13% in CIBMTR. Deaths were recorded at 7% in
both the US and France, 4% in Belgium, and 4.5% in CIBMTR. Event-free survival rates were 85% for the US and France,
86% for Belgium, and 82% for CIBMTR. Patients with chronic GvHD were 12% in the US, 12.6% in France, 20% in Belgium,
and 22% in CIBMTR.
